threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still have that confused look when the bus driver doesn’t stop at the red light; it’s just the way we roll here.
Caught a cheeky glimpse of a DJ setting up a mini rave on the 133 last night. Only in Brixton, eh? Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler chasing pigeons outside the kebab shop, while the old man at the corner laughed and waved. That's Neukölln, life in a snapshot.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find the spontaneous open-air techno parties in forgotten courtyards, echoing the city’s underground spirit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to the smell of burnt popcorn wafting from the taco truck—most just smile and keep walking.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a lady in a tutu chasing after the 21, slipping on wet pavement while juggling coffee and a donut. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the Gaudí hype, but the real magic is in neighborhood fiestas that usually get overshadowed by the tourists.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they stick to the map like it’s a treasure map instead of just walking around and getting lost.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube.' Everyone else just refers to it as 'the Overground' when you're in Hackney.
Heard a guy on the 133 bragging about swiping his mate's seat, only to sit on an old lady's lap after forgetting it’s not rush hour. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home was when I found a spontaneous dance party in a courtyard, complete with an old man playing the accordion. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you spot techno parties in abandoned subway stations at dawn. The echoes of bass and laughter linger long after the rave ends.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to avoid 16th Street BART on Fridays; the echo of broken brakes and bus delays is like clockwork.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus this morning; it was packed, and a guy in a vintage cape was reading Shakespeare out loud. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all sip vermut daily, but most locals save it for Sunday aperitifs with family, not a routine.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by the way they dodge the locals at the market, still browsing like it's a museum instead of a place to haggle.
